axis_responsibility.Agent: Receives declared selected_adapter_ref data per row; adapter split is declared evidence, not provider availability or quality proof.
axis_responsibility.Brick: Owns the preset row shape, repeated work-lens declaration, step_template_ref contract, and optional step_alias as work-composition identity text.
axis_responsibility.Link: Owns gate sufficiency and Movement; fan-in-wait-all and strict-evidence remain declared gate concepts, not support-selected Movement.
axis_responsibility.Support: May materialize declared rows, normalize aliases, reject duplicate node ids, and run checker/smoke evidence without authoring route targets.

checker_or_verifier_plan: Checker-first: add negative fixtures before implementation for repeated template without alias and colliding aliases, expecting ValueError with row and node_id detail.
Add mutation RED that removes or bypasses the node-id collision guard and observes the duplicate/collision fixture passing incorrectly.
Add postmortem-fleet materialize smoke using materialize_building_intent with no walk; assert eight distinct node_ids and the codex/claude selected_adapter_ref split.
Run focused checker/profile coverage for the catalog/template family and collision probes.
Run the full gate from a temp source copy only after implementation.
returned.design_summary: The prescribed design is structurally coherent: add optional row-local step_alias for graph node identity only, require every repeated step_template_ref row to carry a distinguishing alias, retain step_template_ref for template and brick resolution, and fail closed on duplicate node ids with row-specific ValueError detail. The postmortem-fleet preset should mirror recon-fleet while expanding the three QA lens kinds into codex and claude aliased rows, yielding eight graph nodes.
edge_cases: A present step_alias that slugifies to empty should reject.
Two aliases that slugify to the same value should reject.
An alias colliding with another row's unaliased template slug should reject via duplicate node_id.
One aliased and one unaliased row sharing the same step_template_ref should reject because repeated templates require aliases on all repeated rows.
Alias-aware targeting for duplicated templates is not admitted by this task; closure_transition_target_policy should not target duplicated lens templates until a separate key is admitted.
node_reroute_budgets remains keyed by step_template_ref; postmortem-fleet avoids duplicate-budget ambiguity by budgeting only inspect.
invariants: A repeated step_template_ref is admissible only when each repeated row has a non-empty step_alias after normalization.
step_alias affects node identity and labels only.
step_alias must not change step_template_ref, brick_spec_ref, required_return_shape, selected_adapter_ref, Movement, gate refs, or closure policy meaning.
Every materialized node_id must be unique.
Duplicate identity must fail loudly before graph evidence is treated as usable.
postmortem-fleet should materialize to eight distinct nodes: inspect, six lens aliases, and closure.
